75115.subject: Bancor Turns Liquidity Into a Strategy
75115.body:
Hello,

Every week, we take a closer look at one project helping shape the future of digital finance. This week’s spotlight is on Bancor.

Bancor (BNT) is one of the earlier names in decentralised finance, known for helping shape how liquidity pools work in crypto markets. Its role has changed over time, but the core idea remains clear: make on-chain trading possible without depending on traditional order matching.

Today, Bancor’s ecosystem is focused on giving users more control over how liquidity is placed and managed. Through tools such as Carbon DeFi, participants can create automated strategies with custom trading ranges instead of relying only on simple swaps or passive liquidity positions.

This is where Bancor stands apart. Liquidity is not treated as something static. It can be adjusted, structured, and used with specific conditions in mind. For traders and market makers, that means more flexibility in how they interact with on-chain markets.

BNT is currently trading near the $0.2580 level as investors continue to monitor smaller DeFi assets with established histories. Attention around Bancor remains linked to its work on automated liquidity tools and strategy-based trading systems.

Why Bancor Matters Now

*
One of the early protocols behind automated liquidity pools
* Focus on tools for custom on-chain trading strategies
* Supports more flexible liquidity placement
* Connected to the development of DeFi market-making systems

The Strength Behind Bancor

* Long history in decentralised liquidity design
* Ecosystem built around trading, liquidity, and automation
* Carbon DeFi adds strategy-based order functionality
* Positioned within the specialised segment of on-chain liquidity management

Bancor shows how DeFi liquidity has moved beyond basic pool mechanics. As markets become more specialised, protocols that help users control pricing, ranges, and execution rules remain relevant to the deeper structure of decentralised trading.

Explore BNT ( https://app.youhodler.com/wallets/bnt?utm_medium=email )
Explore BNT ( https://app.youhodler.com/wallets/bnt )

The content of this email is for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Please conduct your research before making any investment decisions.

***************************************
Simplifying Crypto Finance for Everyone
***************************************

Trustpilot Rating ( https://www.trustpilot.com/review/youhodler.com )

75112.subject: Jito's execution play
75112.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

Happy Friday! Today we look at Jito's move up Solana's stack and whether JTX can turn its block-building dominance into a product that monetizes execution. Markets stayed risk-off this week, with lending (+8.3%) and Ethereum (+7.4%) the only green sectors as BTC fell 5.0% and nearly everything else bled.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

This week saw broad red across crypto, with lending (+8.3%) and the Ethereum ecosystem (+7.4%) the only sectors in the green. Everything else sold off: BTC -5.0%, DeFi -4.6%, Perps -5.8%, and L2s -17.8%. The pain extended beyond crypto, to gold (-4.3%), the Nasdaq (-3.1%), and the S&P 500 (-1.8%).

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/ad25ad1e-e7fe-4c7b-9cb6-37d76eeec003/image.png?t=1782465030)
Caption: 

The selloff traces to a risk-off June; BTC slid toward $62K on record spot ETF outflows and MicroStrategy stress, with institutions rotating into AI and semiconductor stocks. Against that backdrop, lending's outperformance reads as a defensive rotation into yield-bearing DeFi rather than renewed risk appetite.

Inside the lending index, breadth was thin. AAVE (+9.6%) and KMNO (+7.0%) were the only green components; everything else bled, with FLUID -15.0%, MORPHO -12.8%, COMP -11.2%, and SKY -10.7%. The two leaders alone carried the cohort higher.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eeb3bb12-aff4-4359-8279-deb61728bc4f/image.png?t=1782465030)
Caption: 

On June 19, founder Stani Kulechov pitched V4 as a bridge into Wall Street's securities-financing stack, targeting collateralized lending, repo, and securities lending. Live on mainnet since March 30, V4 has crossed $200M in deposits in under three months on its new hub-and-spoke design, while the Horizon initiative pushes tokenized real-world asset (RWA) lending toward licensed institutions. 

_— _[_Sam_](https://x.com/minnus)

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/76405c7c-c4dc-46df-b706-93d6c4df11a6/0xR_NL_Dashboard_ot_Week.png?t=1781801255)
Caption: 

==Blockworks is ====[now tracking BNB Chain](https://blockworks.com/analytics/bnb)====, one of the largest blockchain ecosystems in crypto with over $18.5 billion in stablecoins and other RWAs.==

==Since launching
